title: Egyptian Smoked Tagine
description: A traditional Egyptian dish featuring ras el hanout, smoky flavors, and a soft, fermented texture. This recipe is an ovo-vegetarian adaptation of the classic recipe.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chickpeas, dried apricots, coriander, cumin, ginger, cayenne pepper, and salt. Stir to combine.
Add the water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the pot with a lid. Simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the ras el hanout and stir to combine.
Simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the liquid has thickened slightly.
Stir in the labneh and cook for 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs or a sprinkle of sumac, if desired.
